title: Knema lunduensis
intro: Knema lunduensis, also known as Lundu nutmeg, is a species of plant in the family Myristicaceae, native to Borneo. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as fever, wounds, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Knema lunduensis include volatile oils, alkaloids, and other compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and poultices to treat various ailments, with the most common preparations being teas and ointments for skin and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Knema lunduensis is a species of plant in the family Myristicaceae. It belongs to the genus Knema, which includes about 100 species of evergreen trees and shrubs.
